title: crocus minimus subvar. corsicus
intro: Crocus minimus subvar. corsicus, also known as Corsican crocus, is a small perennial plant belonging to the iris family and is native to the island of Corsica. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Corsican crocus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Corsican crocus typically involve drying the flowers and using them in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
